name: alert-dialog
title: Alert Dialog
description: A modal that interrupts to demand a decision, built on Base UI's Alert Dialog. Exposes role=alertdialog, traps focus, and returns focus to the trigger. Unlike Dialog it never closes on an outside press and does not close on Escape by default — the user must choose an explicit action. Ships primitive parts (Root, Trigger, Content, Title, Description, Cancel, Action, Footer) plus a convenience AlertDialog with title, description, confirm/cancel labels, onConfirm, and a destructive option that styles the confirm as danger and moves initial focus to the safer Cancel button.
useWhen: Confirming a destructive or irreversible action (delete a permit application, revoke access)
Interrupting a flow to force an explicit confirm/cancel decision before continuing
Any modal where an accidental outside-click or Escape dismissal would be harmful
avoidWhen: General modal content that may be dismissed casually (use a Dialog)
A non-blocking, transient message (use a toast/status region)
A simple on/off setting (use a Switch) or an immediate action with no consequence (use a Button)
Collecting multi-field input as part of a larger task (use a Dialog or a page)

keyboard: Tab / Shift+Tab cycle focus among the dialog's controls only (focus is trapped)
On open, focus moves into the dialog — to the Cancel button when destructive, otherwise to the confirming action
On close, focus returns to the trigger
Enter or Space activates the focused action button
Escape does NOT cancel by default (the interruption must be resolved by an action); set dismissOnEscape to allow Escape to cancel
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (both action buttons are full Commons Buttons)
notes: The popup exposes role=alertdialog with aria-modal=true; the title provides the accessible name via aria-labelledby and the description is linked via aria-describedby.

No outside-press (light) dismiss and no default Escape dismiss: the dialog can only be closed by choosing the Cancel or the confirming action. WAI-ARIA's Alert and Message Dialogs pattern permits Escape as a cancel shortcut, exposed via the opt-in dismissOnEscape prop.
There is intentionally no icon '×' close button; the cancelling action (a Base UI Close) is what lets touch screen-reader users escape the modal.
For a destructive action, initial focus lands on the SAFE Cancel button so an accidental Enter cancels rather than confirms, and the confirming button uses the Button danger variant.

Non-color redundancy: actions are labelled text buttons with a visible border in every state, so intent survives Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode; the backdrop turns fully opaque under prefers-reduced-transparency.
Motion (fade + scale) is gated behind motion-safe and disabled for reduced-motion users.
